title: DIY Personalized Block Photo Holder
summary: A single 2x4 block with three bent-wire photo clips and a personalized name cut from vinyl — a ten-minute build once the block is cut and stained.
description:
About as small a project as this catalog carries: one short length of 2x4, three holes drilled at an angle to hold bent wire or paperclip photo holders, and a personalized name applied with a vinyl cutter. The wood part takes ten minutes; the rest of the character comes from the stain color and the personalization.

Drill the wire holes at a slight backward angle rather than straight down — a photo clip that leans back a few degrees holds a printed photo more securely than one standing bolt upright, since gravity works with the lean instead of fighting a perfectly vertical wire.

tools.note: Match the bit size to your wire or paper clip gauge.

materials.name: Personalized vinyl lettering
materials.note: Cut on a Cricut or similar machine, roughly 10" wide x 2-1/2" tall.

steps.title: Cut and stain the block
steps.body: Cut the 2x4 to 12" long. Stain as desired and let it dry fully before drilling — stain raises the grain slightly, and drilling before staining can leave rougher holes.

steps.title: Drill the wire holes
steps.body: Drill three holes in the top of the block sized to match your wire or paper clip gauge. Angle them back slightly rather than straight down — a leaning clip holds a photo more securely than a vertical one.

steps.title: Add the personalization
steps.body: Apply the vinyl lettering to the front face of the block. A Cricut or similar cutting machine gives the cleanest lettering, but hand-painted lettering or a purchased stencil both work if you don't have one.

images.alt: Personalized 2x4 block photo holder with wire clips
